page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c4a server family. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 CPU architecture, paired with 256.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Operating with dedicated CPU allocation, the c4a-highmem-32 ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource contention from neighboring tenants. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes this instance qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring substantial memory footprints relative to compute capacity, avoiding the need to provision excess vCPUs simply to acquire more RAM. This profile is suited for memory-intensive applications, including medium-sized databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise application servers, and data processing pipelines that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-32 is an x86_64 virtual machine instance from the c4a family, configured with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM. This configuration delivers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, positioning it as a memory-optimized resource. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage options. With dedicated CPU allocation, it provides predictable performance for workloads that demand significant memory capacity without requiring proportional increases in compute power. This makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy applications. It is suited for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching systems, enterprise application hosting, and structured data processing tasks.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 virtual machine featuring thirty-two dedicated vCPUs and two hundred fifty-six gigabytes of RAM for memory-intensive workloads.

No integrated GPU or local storage included
Qualitatively cost-efficient for high-memory, moderate-compute workloads
